Notice Of Motion And Motion For Adherence To Sealing Procedure Pursuant To California Rules Of Court, Rules 2.550(D) And 8.46(D)(6); Request To Direct Clerk To File Exhibit 3 Under Seal
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The matter is not contested, the tentative ruling is adopted.

Motion for Adherence To Sealing Procedure Pursuant To California Rules Of Court, Rules 2.550(d) and 8.46(d)(6); Request To Direct Clerk To File Exhibit 3 Under Seal ("Motion").

The motion is unopposed.

It seeks to seal Exhibit 3 of Ms. Calica's Second Amended Cross Petition Civil Conspiracy; Disinheritance Of An Heir Under California Probate Code 259 et seq; Declaratory Relief; Isolation Of An Elder (Elder Abuse); Elder Abuse, Financial, Neglect And False Imprisonment; Recovery Of Real And Personal Property Inadvertently Excluded From Trust; Elder Abuse, Neglect, And Declaratory; Indemnity/Contribution.

Exhibit 3 is a confidential Report of Suspected Dependent Adult/Elder Abuse, regarding Decedent.

Under Welf. & Inst. Code section 15633, this type of report "shall be confidential and may be disclosed only as provided in subdivision (b)." Under subdivision (b)(1), "Reports of suspected abuse of an elder or dependent adult and information contained in the report may be disclosed only to the following: (1) Persons or agencies to whom disclosure of information or the identity of the reporting party is permitted under Section 15633.5."

The Motion cites Welf. & Inst. Code section 15633.5(a) as the basis for sealing the document. Subdivision (a) lists the people and agencies to whom "Information relevant to the incident of elder or dependent adult abuse shall be given," which includes the probate court. Subdivision (b) states, "The identity of a person who reports under this chapter shall be confidential and disclosed only among the following agencies or persons representing an agency: (1) An adult protective services agency. (2) A long-term care ombudsperson program. (3) A licensing agency. (4) A local law enforcement agency. (5) The office of the district attorney. (6) The office of the public guardian. (7) The probate court. (8) The division. (9) The Department of Financial Protection and Innovation. (10) The Department of Consumer Affairs, Division of Investigation.
(11) Counsel representing an adult protective services agency."

Cal. Rules of Court, rule 8.46 pertains to sealed and confidential records in the California Supreme Court and Courts of Appeal, and is not applicable here, in the trial court.

Pursuant to Cal. Rules of Court, rule 2.550, the court finds facts that establish (1) there exists an overriding interest in maintaining the confidential nature of this document pursuant to Welf.& Inst. Code sections 15633 and 15633.5 that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest in keeping this document confidential per statute supports sealing the record as to this document; (3) the relevant Welf. & Inst. Code statutes will not be observed if the document remains accessible on the public record, thus a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing of only this document, filed as Exhibit 3 of Ms. Calica's Second Amended Cross Petition is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest of keeping this document confidential pursuant to Welf.& Inst. Code sections 15633 and 15633.5.

The court ORDERS Exhibit 3 of Ms. Calica's Second Amended Cross Petition to be sealed.

The moving party shall prepare and provide a proposed order that tracks the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
